stories.headline: Shapiro tells 24 PA utility CEOs the 'regulated utility model is broken'
stories.summary: In an April 29 letter, Gov. Shapiro told CEOs of 24 electric, gas and water utilities that 13 PA utilities sought $975M in 2025 rate increases after earning $1.4B in 2024 profits, and pushed for utilities to bid out their cost of equity through a competitive process — a structural change that would compress regulated returns by several points.

stories.headline: Senate Consumer Protection Committee meets Wednesday at 10:45 a.m.
stories.summary: PA Senate Consumer Protection & Professional Licensure Committee is scheduled to meet May 6 at 10:45 a.m. in Room 461, Main Capitol — agenda not yet posted; worth tracking given Shapiro's parallel pressure on regulated utilities.

stories.headline: Garrity says she'd veto recreational cannabis if elected governor
stories.summary: State Treasurer Stacy Garrity, the probable GOP gubernatorial nominee, told NBC10 Philadelphia she would veto any recreational cannabis legalization bill that reached her desk; the Garrity campaign also launched a $500K opening TV buy with two ads, 'Mr. Incompetent' and 'Service and Leadership.'

stories.headline: DCCC endorsements in contested PA primaries spark intra-Democratic fight
stories.summary: DCCC endorsed candidates still in contested primaries — including bypassing Latino-preferred Carol Obando-Derstine in PA — drawing public criticism from BOLD PAC; PA-7 candidate Lamont McClure said being passed over wouldn't affect his relationship with leadership if he beats GOP Rep. Ryan Mackenzie.

stories.headline: PJM's reopened interconnection queue takes in 800+ projects totaling 220 GW; gas-fired leads at 106 GW
stories.summary: PJM's revamped interconnection queue, now reopened, drew more than 800 projects totaling roughly 220 GW; natural gas accounts for 106 GW — the largest single category — signaling where developers see the easiest path to capacity revenue at current prices.

stories.headline: PJM market monitor opposes 1.3-GW Hull Street/Rockland gas plant deal serving data centers
stories.summary: Monitoring Analytics is pushing back on a 1.3-GW gas plant deal between Hull Street and Rockland Capital, warning the arrangement would 'draw capacity out of the PJM market to serve data centers' — the same load-versus-grid tension at the heart of the Talen-Amazon Susquehanna fight.

stories.headline: Talen-Amazon Susquehanna colocation expected to shift 'front-of-the-meter' this spring
stories.summary: After FERC's November 2024 rejection of the amended ISA, Talen and AWS are moving the 300-MW colo arrangement at Susquehanna to a front-of-the-meter framework — directly relevant to Allegheny Electric Cooperative's 10% stake in the plant — with the transition expected once transmission upgrades finish in spring 2026.

stories.headline: PJM 2027/2028 BRA cleared at $333.44/MW-day cap RTO-wide
stories.summary: PJM's 2027/2028 Base Residual Auction (cleared Dec. 17, 2025) procured 134,479 MW at the FERC-approved $333.44/MW-day cap across all four LDAs, up 1.3% from the 2026/2027 BRA's $329.17 — capacity revenue stays at the cap heading into the next auction.

stories.headline: Indiana primary today is Trump's biggest test of GOP grip in 2026
stories.summary: Indiana Republicans pick state Senate primary winners today after Trump vowed retribution against eight GOP senators who blocked a White House-driven gerrymander; Politico's measure: Trump's side needs to win a majority of the eight target seats to claim victory, with results expected by 8 p.m. ET.

stories.headline: Senate GOP rolls out $72B immigration reconciliation package
stories.summary: Senate Judiciary and HSGAC released text for a $72B reconciliation bill: $38.2B for ICE, $26B for CBP, $5B extra for DHS, $1.5B for DOJ, and $1B for the Secret Service tied to Trump's White House ballroom security; Senate GOP leaders aim to put it on the floor the week of May 18.

stories.headline: Iowa Senate Democratic primary turns nasty ahead of June 2
stories.summary: State Sen. Zach Wahls (Warren-backed) and state Rep. Josh Turek face off in their first head-to-head debate tonight; VoteVets is dropping another $800K on a pro-Turek ad, and the winner takes on GOP Rep. Ashley Hinson in a state Trump carried by 13 in 2024.

stories.headline: Pirates complete sweep of Reds on Cruz's 8th-inning RBI single, 1-0
stories.summary: Oneil Cruz drove home the only run of a Sunday pitchers' duel after rookie Konnor Griffin's two-out double and a Jake Mangum walk; Braxton Ashcraft led the staff and the Pirates (19-16) head to Arizona for a Tue–Thu series at the Diamondbacks.

stories.headline: Steelers give Aaron Rodgers May 18 OTA deadline to decide on 2026
stories.summary: Steelers asked Rodgers for a decision by the May 18 OTA start; in his absence, second-year QB Will Howard sits atop the depth chart ahead of Mason Rudolph at this week's voluntary minicamp under new HC Mike McCarthy.

stories.headline: Penguins enter offseason with 14 UFAs, including Malkin and Skinner
stories.summary: After a first-round playoff exit to the Flyers, the Penguins face decisions on 14 pending UFAs (Malkin, Mantha, Acciari, Dewar, Skinner) and 6 RFAs; Arturs Šilovs' postseason (1.52 GAA, .939 SV%) complicates the goaltending plan.
